good-agent-instructions.content:
# Project Setup

Install with `poetry install`.
Run tests via `pytest`.
Verify types using `mypy`.

## Build

Create packages via `poetry build`.
Show CLI reference: `zfs-replicate --help`.

## Quality

Lint source: `ruff check`.
Auto-format: `ruff format`.
Find orphans: `vulture`.

too-long-instruction.content:
# Verbose Agent Instructions

The agent should first load the project context from all available configuration files and environment variables and then proceed to analyze the entire codebase structure recursively scanning for potential issues including but not limited to dependency conflicts broken imports stale type annotations and inconsistent code patterns before finally generating a comprehensive report that summarizes all discovered problems in a structured format suitable for both human review and automated tooling pipelines.

This sentence is also much too long and tries to cover multiple different concepts all at once instead of being broken down into separate clear and focused instructions that an AI agent can process one at a time without getting confused or losing important contextual information.

## Normal Section

Short and clear.
Another short line.

repeated-instruction.content:
# Repetition Test

The agent must always consult the state registry before modifying
files in the workspace directory. The agent shall verify that all
state registry artifacts are current and consistent with remote
sources before proceeding. The agent also synchronizes the state
registry snapshot at the start of each task execution cycle.

## Another Section

Clean paragraph here.
No repeated words.

human-doc-control.content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to the project. This document helps you set up your
development environment. Follow these steps to get started
quickly and efficiently.

## Prerequisites

Make sure you have Python 3.10 or later installed on your
system. We recommend using Nix for reproducible development
environments, but Poetry also works well for most use cases.

## Installation Steps

Run `poetry install` to set up the project dependencies and
configure your local development environment.
human-doc-control.description: human doc - agent rules don't apply*

code-fence-control.content:
# Configuration

The project uses a JSON schema for settings and a YAML config
for deployment options. Each module validates its own section.

```python
def configure_engine_config_registry(settings):
    """Configure the engine with the provided settings."""
    return settings.apply_to_engine()
```

## Verify

Run `poetry run pytest` to verify all modules pass.

# Template

Replace `<var>` with your identifier.
The `<var>` must be unique.
Use `<var>` when calling endpoints.

## Next Steps

Verify the deployment configuration.
Run the smoke tests.
